name: CNH Industrial N.V.

profile.description: CNH Industrial N.V. is a global capital goods company that designs, manufactures, and distributes agricultural and construction equipment under iconic brands including Case IH, New Holland, and Case Construction. The company also owns Raven Industries (precision agriculture and autonomous systems) and Hemisphere GNSS (satellite positioning technology). Under CEO Gerrit Marx's 'Breaking New Ground' strategy, CNH is integrating AI, autonomy, and precision technology into its legacy iron equipment to modernize farming and construction globally.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): CNH has delivered steady incremental product refreshes — over 15 new tractor launches, 10 harvesting platforms, and 30+ precision tech releases planned through 2027 — but no transformative business model pivot in the last five years [1]. The Iveco Group spinoff in 2022 was a corporate simplification, not a product-driven reinvention. AI Deployment (2/2): CNH's AI Tech Assistant is deployed to hundreds of agricultural and construction dealer groups for real-time diagnostic support, and CEO Marx confirmed every member of the global leadership team is running at least one generative or agentic AI pilot program [1]. The 2026 equipment lineup incorporates AI-powered smart tools and improved automation for farmers [3]. Market Share (1/2): CNH is a meaningful global player in agricultural equipment but trails John Deere in North America and globally. Case IH and New Holland hold solid brand equity but are not category-defining leaders. Pipeline (1/2): Specific product roadmap through 2027 with named precision tech releases and equipment platforms provides tangible pipeline visibility, though revenue contribution from new products is not quantified [1]. R&D Intensity (0/1): R&D spending was approximately 5.4% of net sales in Q4 2025 [4], roughly in line with agricultural equipment peers — not above industry average.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): CNH's purpose of 'Breaking New Ground' connects its equipment business to the generational challenge of feeding a growing world sustainably. CEO Marx has framed food security and farming productivity as existential missions, stating that 'tomorrow's agronomist will be agentic AI helping farmers make bigger decisions better' [1]. The purpose is specific and thoughtful — clearly differentiated from generic industrial mission statements — but has not yet been validated across decades of execution under this framing. Community Impact (1/2): CNH operates employee charitable foundations and community programs across its global footprint, but these are not distinctive in scale or impact relative to peers. Howard W. Buffett serves as Director and Chairperson of the Governance and Sustainability Committee, bringing ESG expertise to the board [5]. Employee Retention (0/2): No specific employee tenure, voluntary turnover, or retention data found after searching. CNH completed strategic leadership appointments in January 2026 (Chief Manufacturing Officer Carlo Materazzo, Chief Legal Officer Britton Worthen) suggesting organizational stability [3]. Insider Buying (1/2): Neutral. CEO Marx received 494,012 shares through RSU vesting in February 2026 with 207,239 shares withheld for taxes — standard equity compensation, not discretionary buying [6]. Marx sold approximately $3.7M in stock in late 2025 [6]. No meaningful net insider buying by executives identified.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics using Growth stock weights (PEG excluded — unavailable). Q1 2026 consolidated revenue of $3.83B was flat versus Q1 2025, beating the $3.71B consensus estimate, but net income collapsed to $10M from $132M a year ago [2]. Full-year 2025 revenue fell 9% to $18.1B. Agriculture adjusted EBIT margin fell to approximately 1% from 5.4% due to negative mix, tariff headwinds (110bps margin impact), and higher R&D/SG&A spending [2]. D/E of 333.72% exceeds the 300% floor, scoring 0.0 on that metric. ROE of 4.84% is well below the 15% target (scores 0.32). Profit margin of 2.13% is near the floor (scores 0.11). Forward P/E of 14.21 is the bright spot — well below the 20 target, scoring a full 1.0. Revenue growth YoY of -0.1% is mid-range (scores 0.66), and 5-year revenue CAGR of approximately 2% scores 0.47. Weighted sum: 3.35 out of max 8.0 = 4.19, rounded to 4. Liquidity: avg daily volume 15.4M × $10.36 = ~$160M, comfortably above the $1M floor.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (7/9): CNH manufactures physical heavy equipment — tractors, harvesters, combines, construction machinery — that AI software cannot replace [2]. The business involves capital-intensive physical products requiring global manufacturing, extensive dealer networks with over 3,500 locations, aftermarket parts and service operations, and a captive financing arm (CNH Industrial Capital). These physical-world operations, deep customer relationships, and regulated financing activities are structurally immune to AI substitution. AI is fundamentally an enabler for CNH's business: precision agriculture tools, AI-powered field analytics, and autonomous equipment capabilities enhance product value and increase the technology content per unit sold. The CTO has noted that 95% of AI pilots fail, indicating careful, disciplined AI deployment [1]. However, CNH does not score at the 8-9 'IMMUNE' level because AI does not directly increase demand for CNH's products the way it does for data center infrastructure — farm equipment demand is driven by commodity prices, farm income, and equipment replacement cycles, not AI adoption. AI Opportunity (1/1): The AI Tech Assistant is deployed across hundreds of dealer groups generating measurable adoption and diagnostic support value. Precision agriculture platforms (Raven, Hemisphere) are shipping AI-enabled products that contribute to technology revenue above base equipment margins [1][3].

infinite_game.summary: CNH under Gerrit Marx has a genuine long-term purpose connecting equipment manufacturing to the existential challenge of global food security. The 'Breaking New Ground' strategy and sustained R&D investment through a historic agricultural downturn reflect infinite game thinking. However, the 60% dividend cut in 2026 and cautious cost management reveal finite-game pressures. The company is evolving from a pure iron manufacturer toward a tech-integrated precision agriculture platform, but this transformation is early-stage and unproven across a full cycle.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): CNH's purpose of 'Breaking New Ground' connects its equipment business to sustainable food production and the challenge of feeding 10 billion people. CEO Marx has framed this as a generational mission, stating that agentic AI will help farmers make bigger decisions better [1]. The purpose is distinctive and shapes R&D investment — CNH maintained 30+ precision tech releases and continued AI pilot programs even during a historic downturn. However, the 'Breaking New Ground' framing is relatively new under Marx's leadership (since mid-2024) and has not been tested across decades or multiple leadership transitions, preventing a full 3-point score.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): CNH completed strategic leadership changes under CEO Marx with key appointments in January 2026 (Chief Manufacturing Officer, Chief Legal Officer) suggesting organizational stability and deliberate team-building [3]. The leadership team appears cohesive in public communications, with Marx referencing collective commitment to the technology transformation. However, the CEO transition from Scott Wine to Marx in July 2024 is recent, and no specific employee retention data, tenure statistics, or cultural evidence beyond standard corporate communications was identified. Average for a global industrial company of this scale.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): CNH explicitly benchmarks against John Deere, AGCO, and Caterpillar and frames its competitive positioning in terms of capability gaps to close rather than rivals to defeat. At the May 2025 Investor Day, Marx emphasized 'expanding Iron + Tech leadership' — language focused on self-improvement and product excellence rather than competitor destruction [3]. Management commentary consistently references learning from competitive dynamics to improve CNH's own precision agriculture offerings and dealer network quality.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): CNH completed the transformative Iveco Group spinoff in January 2022, separating its truck and commercial vehicle business to create a pure-play agriculture and construction equipment company. This was a meaningful strategic simplification that allowed focused investment in precision agriculture technology. The ongoing shift from pure iron manufacturer to a technology-integrated 'Iron + Tech' platform represents incremental but genuine strategic evolution. However, neither move constitutes the kind of dramatic, purpose-driven reinvention that scores full marks — these are rational business restructurings rather than existential pivots driven by a Just Cause.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Despite the worst agricultural equipment downturn in a decade — with industry demand forecast down another 5% in 2026 — CNH maintained its product launch cadence with 15+ new tractor platforms, 10 harvesting products, and 30+ precision tech releases through 2027 [1]. R&D and SG&A spending actually increased in Q1 2026, pressuring short-term margins but building long-term capability [2]. Marx's statement that 'CNH is moving fast to deliver commitments even as markets move slowly' reflects genuine investment through the cycle. However, the 60% dividend cut from $0.25 to $0.10 per share in 2026 [7] and the guided EPS decline to $0.35-$0.45 suggest the company is also managing defensively. One courageous pattern, one cautious counterpoint.

investment_thesis: CNH Industrial is a high-quality agricultural and construction equipment franchise navigating the deepest cyclical trough in a decade with a credible technology-driven recovery story. Q1 2026 revenue of $3.83B beat consensus by 3.2% despite flat YoY growth, and management reaffirmed full-year guidance of $0.35-$0.45 adjusted EPS [2]. The stock trades at 14.2x forward earnings — a significant discount to the 20x target and to peer Deere (trading at ~18x forward) — reflecting trough-level profitability with a 2.13% net margin and 4.84% ROE. CNH's precision agriculture technology stack (Raven autonomous systems, Hemisphere satellite positioning, AI Tech Assistant deployed across dealer networks) positions the company to capture incrementally higher technology revenue per unit as the agricultural equipment cycle turns. At $10.36 per share and $12.85B market cap, patient investors are buying a global #2-3 agricultural equipment franchise at historic trough valuations with visible catalysts for margin recovery.
thesis_continued: The near-term bear case remains severe and is worsening: Q1 2026 net income collapsed 92% to just $10M as agriculture adjusted EBIT margin fell to approximately 1% from 5.4% a year ago [2]. Tariff costs created 110 basis points of EBIT margin headwind in 2025 with 2026 expected to be worse. Construction segment swung to an adjusted EBIT loss of $28M. The 60% dividend cut from $0.25 to $0.10 per share signals management's own concern about near-term cash flow [7]. D/E of 333.72% — significantly above the 300% floor — reflects the heavy balance sheet of a company with a captive financing arm, limiting financial flexibility during the downturn. Industry retail demand is forecast down another 5% in 2026 to historic trough levels, with high-horsepower North American tractors facing 15-20% demand declines [2]. CNH's recovery thesis depends entirely on the agricultural cycle turning in 2027, and if commodity prices or farm income fail to recover, the earnings trough could extend further. Competitive risk from Deere's autonomous and AI-powered equipment rollouts adds pressure on CNH's ability to close the technology gap.
key_catalysts: Agricultural equipment demand recovery beginning in late 2026 or 2027 as dealer inventory destocking completes and farmer income stabilizes — CNH's Q1 2026 revenue beat suggests the bottom may be forming earlier than expected

Precision agriculture technology monetization through the AI Tech Assistant, Raven autonomous platforms, and integrated crop management software driving meaningful recurring revenue above base equipment hardware margins

Margin recovery operating leverage as high-horsepower equipment volumes recover from trough levels — agriculture EBIT margins have compressed from 5.4% to 1%, creating significant snap-back potential when volumes normalize
key_risks: Agricultural equipment demand trough extending beyond 2027 if commodity prices stagnate or farm income fails to recover, delaying the capital expenditure recovery that CNH's entire recovery thesis depends on

Tariff escalation on CNH's globally distributed supply chain (manufacturing in US, UK, Brazil, India, Italy) creating sustained margin headwinds that management has flagged as worsening — 110bps EBIT impact in 2025 with no relief guided for 2026

John Deere's accelerating autonomous and AI-powered equipment rollouts (See & Spray, autonomous tillage) positioning the industry leader further ahead of CNH in precision agriculture software monetization, making it harder for CNH to close the technology gap as the cycle recovers
